Jan 26 2024

<div class="blog-header">

## [The Deutsch Files II](https://nav.al/deutsch-files-ii)

</div>

<div class="content">

[Brett Hall](https://twitter.com/ToKTeacher) and I interview [David
Deutsch](https://twitter.com/DavidDeutschOxf), physicist and author
of [The Beginning of
Infinity](https://www.google.com/search?q=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&safe=active&client=safari&as_qdr=all&channel=aplab&hl=en&ei=4vXnY87tBvmmqtsP6--duAg&oq=the+beginning+of+infinity+book&gs_lcp=ChNtb2JpbGUtZ3dzLXdpei1zZXJwEANKBAhBGABQ2xdYiyVgwSpoAHAAeAGAAQCIAQCSAQCYAQCgAQHAAQE&sclient=mobile-gws-wiz-serp).
Also see [The Deutsch Files I](http://nav.al/deutsch-files-i). The
universality of computation and explanation So let’s go through [The
Fabric of
Reality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Fabric_of_Reality)—the four
theories.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/deutsch-files-ii" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Finding Time to Invest in Yourself](https://nav.al/finding-time)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

If you have to work a “normal job,” take on accountability to build your
specific knowledge

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

This is a transcript of the bonus material at the end of the giant [How
to Get Rich](http://nav.al/rich) episode. A common question we get: “How
do I find the time to start investing in myself? I have a job.” In one
of the tweets from the cutting room floor, you wrote: “You will need to
rent your time to get
started.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/finding-time" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="date">

Jan 13 2020

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Dec 28 2019

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [How to Get Rich](https://nav.al/rich)

</div>

<div class="content">

A collection of all my interviews about my ‘How to Get
Rich’ [tweetstorm](https://twitter.com/naval/status/1002103360646823936). 
Seek Wealth, Not Money or Status Wealth is assets that earn while you
sleep You probably know Naval from his
[Twitter](https://twitter.com/naval) account. We’re going to talk about
his tweetstorm, “[How To Get Rich (without getting
lucky)](https://twitter.com/naval/status/1002103360646823936).”<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/rich" class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<div class="blog-header">

## [Externalities: Calculating the Hidden Costs of Products](https://nav.al/externalities)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

Externalities let you account for the true cost of products by including
hidden costs

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

What’s a mispriced
[externality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Externality)? You mentioned
it on a [previous episode](https://nav.al/capitalism-intrinsic). An
externality is where there’s an additional cost imposed by whatever
product is being produced or consumed, that’s not accounted for in the
price of the product. This can happen for many reasons. Sometimes you
can fix it by putting the cost back into the
price.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/externalities" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Net Present Value: What Future Income Is Worth Today](https://nav.al/npv)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

See what future income is worth today by applying a discount to its
future value

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Let’s talk about [net present
value](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Net_present_value) (NPV). Net
present value is when you say, “That stream of payments I’m going to get
in the future—what’s it worth today?” Here’s a common example: You’re
joining a startup and getting stock options, and the founder says, “This
company is going to be worth \$1 billion, and I’m giving you 0.1% of the
company; therefore, you’re getting \$1 million worth of
stock.”<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/npv" class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

<div class="play-pause">

<div class="playi">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Consumer Surplus: Getting More Than You Paid For](https://nav.al/surplus)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

People are willing to pay more than what companies charge

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

[Consumer surplus and producer
surplus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Economic_surplus) are important
concepts. Consumer surplus is the excess value you get from something
when you pay less than you were willing to pay. I get a lot of joy out
of my morning Starbucks coffee. Obviously I’ve made some money. So if my
coffee cost \$20, I would pay
it.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/surplus" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Price Discrimination: Charge Some People More](https://nav.al/price-discrimination)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

You can charge people for extras based on their propensity to pay

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Are there any other microeconomic concepts, outside of [zero marginal
cost of replication and scale
economies](https://nav.al/business-models), that are important to
understand? Price discrimination is important. It means you can charge
people based on their propensity to pay. Now, you can’t charge people
different amounts just because you don’t like
them.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/price-discrimination"
class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Compounding Relationships Make Life Easier](https://nav.al/relationships)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

Life gets a lot easier when you know someone’s got your back

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Earlier, we talked about [compound interest](https://nav.al/long-term),
but we didn’t dig into it much. Relationships offer a good example of
compound interest. Once you’ve been in a good relationship with somebody
for a while—whether it’s business or romantic—life gets a lot easier
because you know that person’s got your
back.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/relationships" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

<div class="play-pause">

<div class="playi">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Jul 15 2019

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Turn Short-Term Games Into Long-Term Games](https://nav.al/short)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

Improve your leverage by turning short-term relationships into long-term
ones

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Do you want to talk about [Pareto
optimal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pareto_efficiency)? Pareto
optimal is another concept from game theory, along with Pareto superior.
Pareto superior means something is better in some ways while being equal
or better in other ways. It’s not worse in any way. This is an important
concept when you’re
negotiating.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/short" class="post-more-link">More <em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

<div class="play-pause">

<div class="playi">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

<div class="pausei">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

2:41

</div>

</div>

Jul 15 2019

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="blog-item-inner">

<div class="post-meta">

Jul 12 2019

</div>

<div class="blog-header">

## [Schelling Point: Cooperating Without Communicating](https://nav.al/schelling-point)

<div class="blog-subtitle">

People who can’t communicate can cooperate by anticipating the other
person’s actions

</div>

</div>

<div class="content">

Let’s talk about Schelling points. The [Schelling
point](https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/yJfBzcDL9fBHJfZ6P/nash-equilibria-and-schelling-points)
is a game theory concept made famous by Thomas Schelling in his book,
[The Strategy of
Conflict](https://books.google.co.id/books?id=7RkL4Z8Yg5AC&printsec=frontcover&dq=The+Strategy+of+Conflict&hl=en&sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwin7MHuw6njAhX58HMBHe2OC3YQ6AEIKjAA#v=onepage&q=The%20Strategy%20of%20Conflict&f=false),
which I recommend. It’s about multiplayer games where people respond
based on what they think the other person’s response will
be.<span class="post-more-link-before"></span>
<a href="https://nav.al/schelling-point" class="post-more-link">More
<em></em></a>

</div>

<div class="podcast-container-style-2">

<div class="audio-player-container">

<div class="controls">

<a href="#" class="not-smooth play-pause-btn"></a>

<div class="play-pause">

<div class="playi">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

<div class="pausei">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<div class="timer" style="width: 48px">

1:38

</div>

<div class="waves">

<div id="wave-28493318" class="wave">

</div>

<div class="wave-normal">

</div>

<div class="wave-filled">

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div class="dropdown">

Get podcast

<div class="dropdown-menu" aria-labelledby="podcast-open-link">

<a
href="https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/schelling-point-cooperating-without-communicating/id1454097755?i=1000444298974"
target="_blank">Apple</a>
